title: 10 Best Things To Do in Marrakech
author: Merrakechy Tours
excerpt: From the buzzing Djemaa el-Fna square to the serene Majorelle Garden, discover the top 10 experiences every visitor must have in Marrakech.
content:
Marrakech is one of the most vibrant cities in the world. Whether you are visiting for the first time or returning for more, the city never stops surprising you.

## 1. Explore Djemaa el-Fna
The heart of Marrakech, this UNESCO-listed square comes alive at sunset with food stalls, musicians, and storytellers.

## 2. Visit the Majorelle Garden
A stunning blue garden created by French painter Jacques Majorelle and later restored by Yves Saint Laurent.

## 3. Get Lost in the Medina
The ancient walled city is a maze of souks, riads, and hidden courtyards waiting to be discovered.

## 4. Take a Quad Bike Tour
Race through the Palmeraie on a guided quad bike adventure — one of the most thrilling activities near Marrakech.

## 5. Ride a Camel in the Palmeraie
A timeless experience that connects you with Morocco's nomadic Berber traditions.

## 6. Hot Air Balloon at Sunrise
Drift above the Atlas Mountains as the sun rises over the rose-gold Moroccan landscape.

## 7. Day Trip to Ouzoud Waterfalls
Marvel at Morocco's most spectacular waterfalls, 150km from Marrakech.

## 8. Visit the Bahia Palace
A masterpiece of Moroccan architecture with stunning tilework and carved cedar ceilings.

## 9. Explore the Saadian Tombs
A hidden gem rediscovered in 1917, these royal tombs date back to the 16th century.

## 10. Shop in the Souks
From spices to leather goods, the souks of Marrakech offer an unforgettable shopping experience.

title: Marrakech to Sahara Desert: The Ultimate Guide
author: Merrakechy Tours
excerpt: Planning a Sahara desert trip from Marrakech? Here is everything you need to know about routes, costs, and what to expect.
content:
The Sahara Desert is one of the most iconic destinations in Morocco. A trip from Marrakech to the Sahara is a journey through dramatic landscapes, ancient kasbahs, and Berber villages.

## Getting There
The most popular route takes you through the High Atlas Mountains via the Tichka Pass, then through Ouarzazate — the gateway to the Sahara.

## How Long Does It Take?
The drive from Marrakech to Merzouga (the main Sahara dunes) takes approximately 8-10 hours. Most tours are 2-3 days to make the most of the journey.

## What to Expect
Expect stunning scenery, camel rides at sunset, sleeping under the stars in a Berber camp, and sand dunes stretching as far as the eye can see.

## Best Time to Visit
October to April is the best time to visit the Sahara from Marrakech. Summer temperatures can exceed 45°C.

## Book Your Trip
Contact Merrakechy Tours for private and group Sahara tours departing from Marrakech.
